t: Tom says all rectangles are squares. Sara says all squares are rectangles. Who is correct?
e: Sara is correct. Every square IS a rectangle (4 sides, 4 right angles). But not every rectangle is a square -- rectangles can have sides of different lengths.

t: A shape has more than 4 sides and fewer than 7 sides. All its sides are equal length. What shape could it be?
e: More than 4 and fewer than 7 means 5 or 6 sides. With all sides equal, it could be a regular pentagon (5) or regular hexagon (6).

t: You cut a square in half with a straight diagonal line from corner to corner. What 2 shapes do you get?
e: Cutting a square diagonally makes 2 triangles. Each triangle has 3 sides: 2 sides from the square and 1 from the cut.

t: Ben says a triangle can have 4 corners if you make it big enough. What is wrong with his thinking?
o: A triangle always has 3 corners no matter the size
e: Size does not change the number of corners. A triangle ALWAYS has exactly 3 sides and 3 corners, whether it is tiny or huge.

t: How many total sides do a triangle, a square, and a pentagon have together?
e: Triangle = 3, square = 4, pentagon = 5. Add them: 3 + 4 + 5 = 12 sides total.

t: Mia drew a quadrilateral with NO sides equal. Which shape did she definitely NOT draw?
e: A square has all 4 sides equal, so Mia definitely did NOT draw a square. Her shape has no equal sides.

t: If you remove one side from a square and close the shape, what polygon do you get?
e: If you remove one side of a square and connect the remaining corners, you get a triangle with 3 sides.

t: Kai says a circle has 1 side because it has 1 curved edge. Emma says it has 0 sides because sides must be straight. Who is correct for polygons?
e: In geometry, polygon sides must be straight line segments. Since a circle has no straight sides, Emma is correct -- it has 0 polygon sides.

t: A shape has twice as many sides as a triangle. What shape is it?
e: A triangle has 3 sides. Twice as many: 3 x 2 = 6 sides. A hexagon has 6 sides.

t: Sam drew a polygon. It has 2 more sides than a square but 2 fewer sides than an octagon. What did he draw?
e: A square has 4 sides. 4 + 2 = 6. An octagon has 8 sides. 8 - 2 = 6. Sam drew a hexagon (6 sides). Both clues match!

t: Rosa says a rectangle can never be a square. Is she right? Explain.
o: Yes, they are completely different shapes
No -- when all 4 sides are equal, a rectangle IS a square
e: Rosa is wrong. A rectangle CAN be a square! When a rectangle has all 4 sides the same length, it is also a square. A square is a special rectangle.

t: You put 2 identical squares side by side touching along one full side. What shape do you make?
e: Two squares side by side make a rectangle. The new shape is twice as long as it is tall, with 4 sides and 4 right angles.

t: Jaya drew a shape with 3 fewer sides than an octagon. How many corners does her shape have?
e: An octagon has 8 sides. 8 - 3 = 5 sides. A polygon with 5 sides (pentagon) has 5 corners. Sides and corners are always equal!

t: Which statement is FALSE?
e: 'All quadrilaterals are squares' is FALSE. A quadrilateral is any 4-sided shape. Many quadrilaterals (like rectangles and trapezoids) are NOT squares.

t: You cut a rectangle in half with a straight line from the middle of the top to the middle of the bottom. What 2 shapes do you get?
e: Cutting a rectangle straight down the middle gives you 2 smaller rectangles, each with 4 sides and 4 right angles.

t: Leo says: 'I drew a polygon with no corners.' Is this possible?
o: Yes, if it is very small
e: Every polygon must have corners (vertices). If a shape has no corners, it is not a polygon. A circle has no corners, but it is NOT a polygon.

t: A hexagon has how many more sides than a triangle, and how many fewer sides than an octagon?
e: Hexagon = 6. Triangle = 3. 6 - 3 = 3 more. Octagon = 8. 8 - 6 = 2 fewer. So 3 more than a triangle and 2 fewer than an octagon.

t: Ava traced around a square tile 3 times without overlapping. She made a new shape. How many sides could it have if the squares share full sides?
e: Three squares in an L-shape make a polygon with 8 sides. Shared sides become interior and do not count as outside edges.

t: What is the DIFFERENCE between the number of sides on a pentagon and the number of corners on a hexagon?
e: A pentagon has 5 sides. A hexagon has 6 corners. 6 - 5 = 1. The difference is 1.

t: Maya and Alex each draw a quadrilateral. Maya's has all equal sides. Alex's has NO equal sides. Can they both be correct?
o: No -- quadrilaterals must have equal sides
No -- quadrilaterals never have equal sides
Yes -- a quadrilateral just needs 4 sides
Yes -- but only if they are rectangles
e: Yes! A quadrilateral is ANY shape with 4 sides. Maya could draw a square (all equal) and Alex could draw an irregular quadrilateral (no equal sides). Both are valid.
